name: Kotlin compiler
description: The official Kotlin command-line compiler — compiles .kt sources to JVM bytecode, JS, or native binaries
when_to_use: Base dependency for any Kotlin project; usually invoked via Gradle rather than directly

name: ktlint
description: Anti-bikeshedding Kotlin linter with built-in formatter — enforces the official Kotlin coding conventions
when_to_use: Any Kotlin project that wants automated style enforcement and formatting without configuration overhead

name: detekt
description: Static code analyzer for Kotlin focused on code smells, complexity, and maintainability
when_to_use: Production Kotlin codebases that need deeper quality gates than ktlint

name: Gradle
description: The canonical build tool for Kotlin (and modern Java) projects — Kotlin DSL support, incremental builds, rich plugin ecosystem
when_to_use: Every non-trivial Kotlin or Android project; gradle wrapper is standard

name: Dokka
description: API documentation engine for Kotlin — the Kotlin equivalent of javadoc with support for mixed Kotlin/Java projects
when_to_use: For Kotlin libraries that need published API reference docs

name: Kotest
description: Powerful Kotlin testing framework with multiple style DSLs, property-based testing, and data-driven tests
when_to_use: When you want a more Kotlin-idiomatic testing experience than JUnit

name: kotlinx.coroutines
description: Official Kotlin coroutines library for structured concurrency — flows, channels, supervisor scopes
when_to_use: Any Kotlin async code; default choice over threads or callbacks

name: Kotlin Symbol Processing
description: Lightweight compiler plugin API for Kotlin — processes annotations and generates code at compile time, 2x faster than KAPT for annotation processing
when_to_use: When using annotation-heavy libraries like Room, Moshi, or Dagger in Kotlin projects — replaces KAPT with faster compile times

install.command: Add com.google.devtools.ksp Gradle plugin to build.gradle.kts

name: Android Lint
description: Static analysis tool for Android projects — checks Kotlin and Java code for correctness, performance, security, usability, accessibility, and internationalization issues
when_to_use: Before every commit in Android projects — catches Android-specific issues like missing permissions, deprecated API usage, and layout performance problems
what_it_finds: Missing permissions, deprecated APIs, layout performance issues, accessibility problems, hardcoded strings, security vulnerabilities

install.command: Included with Android SDK command-line tools

name: MockK
description: Kotlin-first mocking library — supports coroutines, extension functions, object mocks, and top-level function mocking with a clean DSL that leverages Kotlin language features
when_to_use: When writing Kotlin tests that need mocking — idiomatic alternative to Mockito that handles Kotlin-specific constructs like coroutines and sealed classes

name: Turbine
description: Small testing library for Kotlin Flows — provides a simple API to test Flow emissions, completions, and errors with timeout support and clear failure messages
when_to_use: When testing Kotlin Flow emissions in coroutine-based code — simplifies asserting on sequences of emitted values

name: ktfmt
description: Opinionated Kotlin code formatter by Meta — reformats code to a consistent style with minimal configuration, supporting Google, Meta, and Kotlinlang styles
when_to_use: When you want an opinionated formatter like google-java-format but for Kotlin — stricter than ktlint's formatting

name: Kover
description: Official Kotlin code coverage tool by JetBrains — measures line and branch coverage for Kotlin projects with Gradle and Maven integration, designed specifically for Kotlin
when_to_use: When you need code coverage for Kotlin projects — understands Kotlin-specific constructs better than JaCoCo for accurate coverage metrics

install.command: Add org.jetbrains.kotlinx.kover Gradle plugin to build.gradle.kts

name: Ktor CLI
description: Command-line tool for JetBrains Ktor framework — scaffold new Ktor server projects with selected features, plugins, and build configurations
when_to_use: When bootstrapping new Kotlin server-side applications with Ktor — generates project structure with selected plugins like routing, serialization, and auth

name: Compose Compiler
description: Kotlin compiler plugin for Jetpack Compose — transforms @Composable functions into efficient UI tree operations for Android, desktop, and web targets
when_to_use: Required for any project using Jetpack Compose or Compose Multiplatform — handles the compilation of @Composable annotated functions

install.command: Add org.jetbrains.kotlin.plugin.compose Gradle plugin (Kotlin 2.0+) or set compose compiler version in build.gradle.kts
